name: ArduPilot
description: An open-source autopilot stack used for UAV flight control, mission planning, and vehicle integration across research and commercial drone projects.
whyUseIt: ArduPilot is one of the most accessible ways to learn how aircraft autonomy, mission execution, and ground-station workflows fit together in practice.
fieldPosition: UAV controls, mission execution, and integration testing
engineerWorkflow: Configure the vehicle, tune basic control loops, define missions, run simulation or field tests, and review flight logs to improve behaviour.
managerExpects: A safe integration plan, parameter discipline, and clear boundaries between experiment code and operationally trusted configurations.
demoDescription: Set up a simple survey mission in simulation, then inspect the log to understand how the vehicle tracked waypoints and handled disturbances.
beginnerMistakes: Changing many parameters at once and losing track of cause and effect
Skipping simulation and safety checks before field testing
